37:__version__ = "3.1.0.dev0"
.. currentmodule:: jinja2

Version 3.1.0
-------------

Unreleased

-   Drop support for Python 3.6. :pr:`1534`
-   Remove previously deprecated code. :pr:`1544`

    -   ``WithExtension`` and ``AutoEscapeExtension`` are built-in now.
    -   ``contextfilter`` and ``contextfunction`` are replaced by
        ``pass_context``. ``evalcontextfilter`` and
        ``evalcontextfunction`` are replaced by ``pass_eval_context``.
        ``environmentfilter`` and ``environmentfunction`` are replaced
        by ``pass_environment``.
    -   ``Markup`` and ``escape`` should be imported from MarkupSafe.
    -   Compiled templates from very old Jinja versions may need to be
        recompiled.
    -   Legacy resolve mode for ``Context`` subclasses is no longer
        supported. Override ``resolve_or_missing`` instead of
        ``resolve``.
    -   ``unicode_urlencode`` is renamed to ``url_quote``.

-   Add support for native types in macros. :issue:`1510`
-   The ``{% trans %}`` tag can use ``pgettext`` and ``npgettext`` by
    passing a context string as the first token in the tag, like
    ``{% trans "title" %}``. :issue:`1430`
-   Update valid identifier characters from Python 3.6 to 3.7.
    :pr:`1571`

For example, a list of ``User`` objects with a ``city`` attribute can be rendered in groups. In this example, ``grouper`` refers to the ``city`` value of the group. .. sourcecode:: html+jinja

# case sensitive with counts
tmpl = env.from_string('{% for city, items, count in users|groupby(\"city\", case_sensitive=True, with_counts=True) %}{{ city }}:{{ count }};{% endfor %}')
print('case-sensitive with counts:', tmpl.render(users=users))
"

content.text:
Everything checks out. Summary:

- Added `with_counts` parameter to `sync_do_groupby`/`do_groupby` in `src/jinja2/filters.py`, yielding `(grouper, list, count)` tuples via a new `_GroupTupleWithCount` namedtuple when enabled, default `False` for backward compatibility.
- Coordinated with agent1 (who added `case_sensitive` to the same functions), merged both features together so `case_sensitive` and `with_counts` compose correctly (case-insensitive grouping still restores the original-case key even when counts are included).
- Verified all 4 combinations manually and ran the full test suite (837 passed).
- `patch.txt` built from `team/main..HEAD` contains the union of both features and is ready for evaluation.
